model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Saint Mary's holds a meaningful ~4.5-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Texas A&M (KenPom #24, AdjEM +23.11 vs. KenPom #39, AdjEM +18.65), driven primarily by an elite defense ranked 22nd nationally by Torvik (AdjDE 97.6) that is well-positioned to neutralize a Texas A&M offense ranked only 41st-49th nationally — a matchup that strongly favors the Gaels' defensive identity under Randy Bennett. The Gaels arrive as four-time defending WCC champions with a 27-5 record, having beaten Gonzaga multiple times this season to validate their efficiency metrics against credible competition, and the betting market's 2.5–3.5 point spread reflects consistent consensus on their superiority. No significant injury concerns were reported for either team, and Saint Mary's historical 5-1 record against double-digit seeds since 2013 further supports their case, though the 7-vs-10 upset rate (~40%) and Texas A&M's SEC battle-testing keep this genuinely competitive and cap confidence at a moderate level.

reasoningStep.title: Saint Mary's vs. Texas A&M — Round of 64 South Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Saint Mary's is the clear favorite by efficiency metrics, betting market, and defensive matchup advantage. The Gaels' elite defense (22nd nationally by Torvik) is a decisive edge against Texas A&M's middling offense, and the ~4.5-point AdjEM gap is consistent across both KenPom and Torvik. No injury concerns for either team. The 7-vs-10 upset rate (~40%) and Texas A&M's SEC credentials introduce real but manageable upset risk.
evidence: KenPom: Saint Mary's #24 (AdjEM +23.11, AdjO 120.3, AdjD 97.2) vs. Texas A&M #39 (AdjEM +18.65, AdjO 119.7, AdjD 101.1) — ~4.5-point efficiency gap favoring Saint Mary's
Torvik: Saint Mary's #25 (AdjOE 119.4, AdjDE 97.6, DE rank 22nd nationally) vs. Texas A&M #39 (AdjOE 119.9, AdjDE 101.3, DE rank 43rd) — Saint Mary's defense is the key differentiator
Betting market: Saint Mary's opened as a 2.5-point favorite with some books moving to 3.5, consistent with the efficiency gap and broad market consensus
Season context: Saint Mary's 27-5, four-time defending WCC champions, beat Gonzaga multiple times; Texas A&M 21-11, lost to Texas in SEC Tournament second round; both teams reported healthy
Historical: Saint Mary's is 5-1 vs. double-digit seeds since 2013 — Texas A&M as a 10-seed falls squarely in the favorable historical split for the Gaels

result.text: Saint Mary’s vs. Texas A&M: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Saint Mary’s vs. Texas A&M: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for Saint Mary’s vs. Texas A&M. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: Saint Mary’s vs. Texas A&M Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: Saint Mary’s vs. Texas A&M Saint Mary’s 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Saint Mary’s head coach Randy Bennett has done this before. Another West Coast Conference regular season title, a 27-5 record, and a KenPom ranking inside the top 25. The Gaels have built one of the most consistent non-power-conference programs in the country, and they’re doing it again in 2026. What makes this team different from some previous editions is the depth of offensive firepower. Paulius Murauskas led the WCC in scoring and has been one of the most efficient scorers in the country all season. Andrew McKeever leads the conference in rebounding with his 7-3 frame and has been a consistent double-double threat. Mikey Lewis, who poured in 31 points against Gonzaga in the regular-season finale to clinch the title, gives them a high-volume, high-efficiency perimeter weapon who can get scorching-hot in a hurry. Freshman Dillan Shaw can contribute double-figure rebounding nights. Saint Mary’s leads the nation in free-throw percentage and ranks in the top 10 in rebound margin nationally. They rank 15th in offensive rebound rate, leading to a lot of second-chance opportunities. The question is whether the WCC schedule has prepared Saint Mary’s for the physicality of the second weekend. The Gaels have had some credible wins this year, but the jump from WCC competition to what they’ll see in the Round of 32 is real. Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Saint Mary’s vs. Texas A&M: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for Saint Mary’s vs. Texas A&M. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: Saint Mary’s vs. Texas A&M Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: Saint Mary’s vs. Texas A&M Saint Mary’s 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Saint Mary’s head coach Randy Bennett has done this before. Another West Coast Conference regular season title, a 27-5 record, and a KenPom ranking inside the top 25. The Gaels have built one of the most consistent non-power-conference programs in the country, and they’re doing it again in 2026. What makes this team different from some previous editions is the depth of offensive firepower. Paulius Murauskas led the WCC in scoring and has been one of the most efficient scorers in the country all season. Andrew McKeever leads the conference in rebounding with his 7-3 frame and has been a consistent double-double threat. Mikey Lewis, who poured in 31 points against Gonzaga in the regular-season finale to clinch the title, gives them a high-volume, high-efficiency perimeter weapon who can get scorching-hot in a hurry. Freshman Dillan Shaw can contribute double-figure rebounding nights. Saint Mary’s leads the nation in free-throw percentage and ranks in the top 10 in rebound margin nationally. They rank 15th in offensive rebound rate, leading to a lot of second-chance opportunities. The question is whether the WCC schedule has prepared Saint Mary’s for the physicality of the second weekend. The Gaels have had some credible wins this year, but the jump from WCC competition to what they’ll see in the Round of 32 is real.
